2026.methodology: Data reflects the official 2026 budget proposal submitted to the Seoul Metropolitan Council. The 7.0% increase (approx. 2.51 billion USD) compared to 2025 is driven by a projected 2.1% GDP recovery and enhanced local corporate tax performance. Spending allocations prioritize the 'Accompanying the Vulnerable' initiative (approx. 11.56 billion USD) and infrastructure upgrades for climate resilience. All figures converted to USD.

sources: South Korea Ministry of the Interior and Safety

local_taxes.name: Local Taxes and Shared National Taxes

bonds_carryover.name: Municipal Bonds and Previous Year Surplus

environment_safety.name: Climate Change Response and Public Safety

2025.methodology: The 2025 total budget of 35.56 billion USD reflects an expansionary fiscal policy focused on social safety nets and the 'Great Sunset Han River' project. Revenue projections are based on expected 3.2% growth in local corporate tax and adjusted real estate transaction taxes. Expenditures are categorized by the functional budget classification system of the SMG.

sources: Ministry of Economy and Finance (MOEF) South Korea

2024.methodology: Data extracted from the official 2024 Budget Plan of the Seoul Metropolitan Government. Revenue total (33.88 billion USD) reflects the final approved fiscal appropriation including local tax projections and central government transfers. Spending categories are aggregated based on functional classification of municipal expenditures.
